tokens: Tony, on the rim of that little crater, as I walked through there, there was a...Underneath the regolith there was a white area.
I kicked up some very white soil, about 3 centimeters down.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I'll get it; let me have it.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Watch out for those cables behind you.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Chuckles as he examines the anchor) That's pretty good.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I stuffed the geophone stake in, by just pushing on it about, oh, a...about a foot.
Y'all better think about that two-tenths cone penetration at Stop 10.
It looks like the five(-tenths cone) would be the best, all the way out.
I think I'll just go right up to the hilt with the two.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I was just waiting to get the dumb heat thing.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: John, things just stand up...The wires (and) everything just stands up off the ground about 6 inches here.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We should have (garbled)...We never thought of that.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: This is gonna be the trick of the week.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Not only has he gotten the geophone in the ground, he's got it buried in the ground.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: This is going to be a lot easier than carrying that big...other backpack.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Keep an eye on this, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Now I'm going out and parallel these (Rover) tracks.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Let me make sure (the cable loops in the anchor don't slip in tension, as per the reminder on
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: You don't need the hammer; we don't need the hammer.

tokens: The other stake is over there.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Sounds like the (Far UV) camera re-moding.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Make sure that it's not pulling too hard on that wire back there.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I think you need a (Hasselblad) camera (to document the geophone deployment).

tokens: Yeah, I'm going to get one.
I'll go run get it now.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Watch (me) and see where this line comes out.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (To Houston) I'd better do that, Tony.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We know we need a...(Hearing Tony) Okay.
(To John) You're walking a little bit sideways, John; you're pulling against it with your left side.
The geophone's still in, and the stake is looking good.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Trouble is, the average guy doesn't realize how far 100 meters is.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Exaggerating) Roger; 2 inches at a time like that, it's a long way.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Ah, the footprints on the Moon!
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: They'll probably erode away in about 4 billion years.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (Looking back) Man, that's a long way, John!
Stake is still up (near the Central Station).
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (I'll) run around, and get in front of you here.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: That's right, we have to put that (geophone) in before we put the stake (a second anchor) in.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Yeah, I can't do anything till I get that in.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, that one's buried too, Tony.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: No, you want it this way, don't you?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: You're right about that...We're all right.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Get the flag open, so we can see it.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I'm going to get a camera.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Right here on this side, your right (side).
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: It's a far cry from that...(Pause)
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: They got to change out your air hose.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Where's the...Hey, Tony, where is the Bendix air compressors?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: It doesn't look right with them not out there.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: I'll tell you this one-sixth gravity feels a lot better (than training in one g in Florida).
(Pause) Tony, when I first started with that jack, the ground was so soft, I thought the thing had failed like it did in the last training, but it had not.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Well, I'll say one thing: the force to deploy this thing, I can sense, is exactly the same as it was in one g.
And I keep wondering when the power wire is going to bust (from the tension in it).
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: The back of...The bottom of the bit is (capped) with Bravo.
And I'm going back out and take some pictures.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: John has disappeared over the horizon!!
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: John, how about an EMU status check?
You pulled out your pin on your purge valve.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: 70...(Counting marks on the pressure gauge) 50, 60, 73 percent is what I read.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Well, you still must be locked.
I picked it up, and it's under your seat.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (It was) right next to the Rover.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Wait a minute until I stick up the flag.

tokens: I guess that's a pretty straight line, Charlie.

tokens: I haven't got the flag up.

tokens: ...to put John's (purge valve locking) pin back in.

tokens: All right, I'll go get it; just a moment.

tokens: Ready for the first ALSEP (means the first thumper shot).
It's set on zero; we'll go to 1.
Ready for the first geophone to fire.

tokens: Tony, the PSE skirt has some dust kicked on it - on the north side a little bit.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I guess we're going to have to have you stand still for a little bit here while we calibrate something.

tokens: It must have pulled out on the geophone somehow.
Where did you pick it up, Charlie?

tokens: Right next to your...to the Rover.

tokens: I'm all clear on the flags, my pressure gauge is 3.8.

tokens: Nope; let me try that again.

tokens: We got an outstanding signal here; it looks great!

tokens: Ah, you got a beauty there, John.

tokens: We would like for you to look at the end of that heat flow cable that was broken off and tell us how far from the heat central station it broke, and also describe the end of the broken cable.

tokens: Stand by a minute, let me stand up here.

tokens: You ready to hold still, Charlie?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Let me get up, and then I'll hold still.

tokens: Sound doesn't travel too good in a vacuum.
Okay, Charlie, I'm going to the next station now.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, Tony, the...I'm going to be still, John, so you just go ahead.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, Tony, the cable is broken off right at the connector.

And there's about an inch and a half of silver material right in my hand at the end of the broken piece, and, as I look at the cable it's right at the...(correcting himself) the connector, rather, it's (severed) right at the connector (at the base of the Central Station).
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (To Charlie) How does the connector look?
Is there any damage on it?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Tony, there's no damage on the connector.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (To Houston) Am I holding still long enough?
transcript.speaker: Young

transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Pause) Hey, Tony, did you get my question about am I holding still long enough?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Yes, you're holding still long enough.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (Facing the Rover, unable to see John) (Are) you moving, John?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Houston, do you want...Answer me, do you want me to do one by the geophone, or do I skip that one?
There's a white thing by it.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: The one right by the central geophone; yes, we do want.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: The one we don't want is the next one along the cable.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay, we're sure getting good signals here, John.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: The next one we skip because it has got a black wire on it.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: It's firing, babe, I'll tell ya.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Not great it's getting you all dirty; great it's working.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay, Tony, one of the arms of the LSM, when I pull the sunshade, the arm to the northwest (means southwest) (pause) does not lock.

tokens: But I can't unravel this sunshade; it pulls up the arms.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, if you can't get that out there...
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Charlie, I guess we'd just like you to leave the sunshade alone; just let it hang there.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: It was wrapped around a couple of times.
I didn't touch (it more than a) little bit, or disturb (the) level.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And, Charlie, while you're taking the pictures and all, verify the area around the Central Station is policed up.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: And we're still getting good signals.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Coming up on the last one.
(Pause) And I see the black thing (halfway between locations 18 and 19); skipping that one.
Going up here by the Central Station.
(Pause) Going to fire it at the white line next to the first geophone.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, and, after you fire this one, we'd like you to stand still until we give you the Go.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (Amazed at how long they are having to stand still) Houston, you got to be kidding.
Was the signal reverberating that long?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Tony, ain't there some way we can fix that heat flow?

tokens: We suggest you just not worry about it there.

tokens: Okay, the old Central Station is all cleaned up.

tokens: And it was right out here by the drill.

tokens: Let me get this thing (meaning the purge valve pin) in, and I'll go back and start on that heat flow.

tokens: What is it you want to do?

tokens: Am I in focus right here, Tony?

tokens: I'll go put these stubs...(correcting himself) these cores, back out there.

tokens: Do you want me to save the drill?

tokens: Yeah, why don't you just leave it setting there?

tokens: In case you guys come up with something?

tokens: Okay, John, I'm going to start configuring over here for some geology.

tokens: Be there as soon as I can, Charlie.

tokens: You want me to come help, John?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Well, Charlie, it's a question...I can't get this pin around this angle in here.

tokens: Well, if it won't come out, I guess we're stuck with it.

tokens: Well, I don't see any way with my
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: The mortar package is in place.
